heading: Museum services activities

L. 111–340, § 303(2)(C)(ii)2003—, amended section catchline and text generally. Prior to amendment, section contained provisions which in subsec. (a) authorized the Director to make grants to museums to pay for the Federal share of the cost of increasing and improving museum services, in subsec. (b) authorized the Director to enter into contracts and cooperative agreements with appropriate entities to pay for the Federal share of enabling the entities to undertake projects designed to strengthen museum services, in subsec. (c) established the Federal share, and in subsec. (d) required the Director to establish procedures for reviewing and evaluating grants, contracts, and cooperative agreements entered into under this subchapter.Pub. L. 108–81, § 303

Effective Date of 2015 AmendmentAmendment by effective , except with respect to certain noncompetitive programs and competitive programs, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 114–95section 5 of Pub. L. 114–95section 6301 of this titleDec. 10, 2015

children.text: supporting museums in providing learning and access to collections, information, and educational resources in a variety of formats (including exhibitions, programs, publications, and websites) for individuals of all ages;

supporting museums in building learning partnerships with the Nation’s schools, including tribal schools, and developing museum resources, capabilities, and programs in support of State and local efforts to offer a well-rounded educational experience to all students;

children.label: A
children.text: provide optimal conditions for storage, exhibition, and use;

children.label: B
children.text: prepare for and respond to disasters and emergency situations;

children.label: C
children.text: establish endowments for conservation;

children.label: D
children.text: curate, stabilize, and organize object-related information; and

children.label: E
children.text: train museum staff in collections care;

supporting efforts at the State level to leverage museum resources, including statewide assessments of museum services and needs and development of State plans to improve and maximize museum services through the State;

creating shared technology tools and data resources, and developing museum staff training plans to improve and maximize museum services through organizations such as museum associations, professional networks, community-based organizations, and foundations, and through other collaborative mechanisms;

children.label: A
children.text: libraries;

children.label: B
children.text: schools;

children.label: C
children.text: institutions of higher education;

children.label: D
children.text: international, Federal, State, regional, tribal, and local agencies or organizations;

children.label: E
children.text: nongovernmental organizations;

children.label: F
children.text: other community organizations; and

children.label: G
children.text: other relevant research and policy organizations;

encouraging the use of new and emerging technologies and media, including new ways to disseminate information, engage varied audiences in experiential learning, and support teachers and students to enhance access to and engagement with museum collections, programs, and services;

supporting museums in developing, carrying out, and providing programs and services that meet the needs of communities throughout the Nation, including people of diverse geographic, cultural, and socioeconomic backgrounds, individuals with disabilities, residents of rural and urban areas, Native Americans, military families, veterans, and caregivers, and for State institutions;

supporting professional development and technical assistance programs to enhance museum operations, and the skills of museum staff, at all levels, and to support the development of the next generation of museum leaders and professionals, in order to ensure the highest standards in all aspects of museum operations;
supporting museums in research, program evaluation, and the collection and dissemination of information to museum professionals and the public;
encouraging, supporting, and disseminating model programs of museum and library collaboration;
enabling museums to serve as anchor institutions to support community revitalization and the creation of opportunities for advancement; and
developing public and private partnerships with other agencies and community-based organizations to expand and enhance museum services.

children.heading: 50 percent
children.label: 1 50 percent
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the Federal share described in subsection (a) shall be not more than 50 percent.

children.heading: Greater than 50 percent
children.label: 2 Greater than 50 percent
children.text: The Director may use not more than 20 percent of the funds made available under this subchapter for a fiscal year to enter into arrangements under subsection (a) for which the Federal share may be greater than 50 percent.

children.heading: Operational expenses
children.label: 3 Operational expenses
children.text: No funds for operational expenses may be provided under this section to any entity that is not a museum.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director shall establish procedures for reviewing and evaluating arrangements described in subsection (a) entered into under this subchapter.

children.heading: Grant distribution
children.label: 2 Grant distribution
children.text: In awarding grants, the Director shall take into consideration the equitable distribution of grants to museums of various types and sizes and to different geographic areas of the United States.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director may use not more than 10 percent of the funds appropriated to carry out this subchapter for technical assistance.

children.heading: Individual museums
children.label: B Individual museums
children.text: Individual museums may receive not more than 3 technical assistance awards under subparagraph (A). Subsequent awards for technical assistance shall be subject to review outside the Institute.

children.heading: Services for Native Americans
children.label: d Services for Native Americans
children.text: From amounts appropriated under , the Director shall reserve 2.25 percent to award grants to, or enter into contracts or cooperative agreements with, Indian tribes and organizations that primarily serve and represent Native Hawaiians (as defined in ), to enable such tribes and organizations to carry out the activities described in subsection (a).section 9176 of this titlesection 7517 of this title
